substances.name: Bisoprolol
toxicities: Beta blocker overdose can cause bradycardia, hypotension, AV block, cardiogenic shock, bronchospasm, hypoglycemia masking, seizures, and cardiac arrest.
Plasma concentrations are not routine clinical monitoring targets; acute toxicity assessment is clinical and ECG/hemodynamic based.
substances.summary: 比索洛尔是 β1 选择性阻滞剂。本条目用于药代/药效时间进程学习和资料索引，不构成处方、诊断或自行调整剂量建议。

indications: Hypertension
Selected heart failure and angina contexts depending on local label
majorWarnings: Abrupt withdrawal can worsen angina or precipitate cardiac events in susceptible patients.
Bradycardia, hypotension, heart block, bronchospasm, and masking of hypoglycemia symptoms are clinically relevant safety concerns.
majorInteractions: Other agents that slow heart rate or AV conduction may increase bradycardia or conduction risk.
Insulin or oral hypoglycemics may have masked adrenergic symptoms of hypoglycemia.

notes: Tmax is a plasma concentration parameter and is not necessarily the time of maximal clinical effect.
The chart tail uses half-life as a clearance-oriented visual estimate when no explicit duration of action is encoded.

tdm.monitoringType: not routinely monitored by plasma concentration
tdm.reason: Clinical use is usually guided by heart rate, blood pressure, symptoms, ECG context, organ function, and interacting drugs rather than plasma concentration.

Exposure differs by CYP2D6 metabolizer status.

Other agents that slow heart rate or AV conduction may increase bradycardia or conduction risk.

notes: Tmax and half-life vary with CYP2D6 phenotype and interacting drugs.

toxicities: Overdose can cause excessive beta blockade and alpha blockade with bradycardia, hypotension, heart block, bronchospasm, and shock.
Clinical management is guided by symptoms, ECG, hemodynamics, and supportive care rather than routine plasma concentration.
substances.summary: 拉贝洛尔兼具 α1 阻滞和 β 阻滞作用。本条目用于学习药代时间进程、血压/心率监测和相互作用索引。

indications: Hypertension
majorWarnings: Can cause symptomatic hypotension, bradycardia, heart block, bronchospasm, and hepatic injury in rare cases.
Abrupt withdrawal of beta blockers can worsen ischemic symptoms in susceptible patients.
majorInteractions: Other agents that lower blood pressure or slow AV conduction can increase hypotension, bradycardia, or conduction risk.
Insulin or oral hypoglycemics may have masked adrenergic symptoms of hypoglycemia.
monitoring: Liver injury symptoms when clinically indicated

notes: Peak plasma level is not the same concept as maximal blood-pressure response.
Food and hepatic function can affect exposure.

substances.name: Enalapril
commonNames: 血管紧张素转换酶抑制剂
C09 肾素-血管紧张素系统药物
Agents acting on the renin-angiotensin system
toxicities: ACE inhibitor toxicity or excessive effect can include hypotension, renal function deterioration, hyperkalemia, and angioedema.
Monitoring is clinical and laboratory based rather than plasma concentration based.
substances.summary: 依那普利是 ACE 抑制剂，口服后转化为活性代谢物 enalaprilat。本条目区分活性代谢物达峰和临床降压监测。

drugClass: 血管紧张素转换酶抑制剂
C09 肾素-血管紧张素系统药物
Agents acting on the renin-angiotensin system
indications: Hypertension
Heart failure and selected left ventricular dysfunction contexts depending on local label
contraindications: Pregnancy-related contraindications vary by label and jurisdiction
majorWarnings: Can cause angioedema, fetal toxicity, hypotension, renal impairment, and hyperkalemia.
Renal function and potassium changes are clinically important.
Hyperkalemia and renal-function worsening can occur.
Pregnancy exposure can cause fetal toxicity; follow the product boxed warning or local labeling.
majorInteractions: Potassium supplements, potassium-sparing diuretics, or salt substitutes can increase hyperkalemia risk.
NSAIDs may reduce antihypertensive effect and increase renal risk in susceptible patients.
Dual renin-angiotensin system blockade can increase renal impairment, hypotension, and hyperkalemia risk.

tmax.note: Peak serum concentration of active enalaprilat after oral enalapril.

eliminationHalfLife.note: Effective half-life for accumulation of enalaprilat after multiple doses.

notes: The encoded Tmax refers to enalaprilat, the active metabolite.
Renal impairment can increase enalaprilat exposure and delay time to steady state.

tdm.monitoringType: not routinely monitored by plasma concentration
tdm.reason: Clinical use is guided by blood pressure, renal function, potassium, symptoms, and local labeling rather than plasma concentration.

substances.name: Verapamil
commonNames: 非二氢吡啶类钙通道阻滞剂
toxicities: Verapamil toxicity can include bradycardia, AV block, hypotension, hyperglycemia, shock, and severe conduction disturbances.
Overdose assessment is clinical and ECG/hemodynamic based; plasma concentration is not a routine management target.
substances.summary: 维拉帕米是非二氢吡啶类钙通道阻滞剂，具有传导抑制和负性肌力风险。本条目按普通制剂和缓释制剂区分时间进程。

drugClass: 非二氢吡啶类钙通道阻滞剂
indications: Hypertension
Angina
Selected arrhythmia or rate-control contexts depending on formulation and label
contraindications: Sick sinus syndrome or AV block without a functioning pacemaker in relevant labels
Severe left ventricular dysfunction in relevant labels
majorWarnings: Can prolong AV nodal conduction and cause bradycardia or AV block.
May worsen heart failure in susceptible patients.
Bradycardia, AV block, and worsening heart failure can occur in susceptible patients.
majorInteractions: Beta blockers and digoxin may have additive effects on cardiac conduction.
Verapamil is involved in CYP3A4/P-gp interactions and can increase exposure of susceptible substrates.
Some statins can have increased exposure and myopathy risk with verapamil.
CYP3A inhibitors or inducers may alter exposure; verify the formulation-specific label.
monitoring: ECG or conduction symptoms when clinically indicated

notes: Half-life can increase during repeated dosing or titration.
PR interval effects and blood pressure response are clinical/pharmacodynamic concepts, not identical to Tmax.
Extended-release verapamil has formulation-specific absorption and should not be collapsed with immediate-release entries.

tdm.monitoringType: not routinely monitored by plasma concentration
tdm.reason: Clinical monitoring usually focuses on heart rate, blood pressure, ECG/conduction, heart failure symptoms, and interactions.

commonNames: 二氢吡啶类钙通道阻滞剂
toxicities: Excessive nifedipine effect or overdose can cause hypotension, reflex tachycardia or bradycardia, shock, and metabolic disturbances.
Immediate-release and extended-release products have different time-course profiles and should not be treated as interchangeable timeline entries.

indications: Hypertension
Angina depending on formulation and local label
majorWarnings: Can cause symptomatic hypotension, edema, flushing, headache, and reflex tachycardia.
Immediate-release formulations have safety concerns in some cardiovascular contexts; follow local approved labeling.
Immediate-release and extended-release nifedipine must not be treated as interchangeable time courses.
majorInteractions: Strong CYP3A inhibitors can increase nifedipine exposure.
Other antihypertensives can increase blood pressure-lowering effect.
CYP3A inhibitors or inducers may alter exposure; verify the formulation-specific label.

onset.note: DailyMed describes drug detectable in serum 10 minutes after oral administration.

notes: Immediate-release nifedipine has a substantially faster plasma peak than extended-release tablets.
Do not infer prescribing guidance from this educational timeline.
Labels describe a second small peak or shoulder around 6-12 hours for some extended-release tablets.

Edema depending on label and clinical context
majorWarnings: Can cause electrolyte imbalance, volume depletion, renal function changes, and hyperuricemia.
Photosensitivity and metabolic changes may occur.
Dehydration, volume depletion, electrolyte abnormalities, and renal-function worsening can occur.

Drugs affecting potassium can alter hypokalemia or hyperkalemia risk depending on combination.
monitoring: Uric acid and gout symptoms when relevant

durationOfAction.note: DailyMed describes activity persisting up to 24 hours.

notes: Diuretic peak effect and plasma Tmax are related but distinct concepts.
Electrolyte and renal monitoring are more clinically relevant than routine concentration monitoring.

tdm.reason: Clinical monitoring focuses on blood pressure, volume status, renal function, and electrolytes.

Hypertension

monitoring: Uric acid and gout symptoms when relevant

substances.name: Rivaroxaban
toxicities: Clinically important toxicity is bleeding; risk depends on renal/hepatic function, dose context, age, interacting drugs, and invasive procedures.
Routine plasma concentration monitoring is not typical, but anti-Xa or drug-calibrated assays may be used in selected clinical scenarios depending on local availability.
substances.summary: 利伐沙班是直接口服抗凝药和 Xa 因子抑制剂。本条目区分说明书药代参数与抗凝活性/出血风险监测。

indications: Stroke/systemic embolism risk reduction in selected atrial fibrillation contexts
Treatment or prevention of venous thromboembolism depending on label
Selected coronary or peripheral artery disease contexts depending on label
majorWarnings: Bleeding can be serious or fatal.
Premature discontinuation can increase thrombotic risk in some contexts.
Spinal/epidural hematoma risk is relevant with neuraxial anesthesia or spinal puncture.
majorInteractions: Combined P-gp and strong CYP3A inhibitors can increase exposure.
Combined P-gp and strong CYP3A inducers can decrease exposure.
Other anticoagulants, antiplatelets, or NSAIDs can increase bleeding risk.
monitoring: Anti-Xa or drug-calibrated assays only in selected scenarios

eliminationHalfLife.note: DailyMed describes terminal half-life of 5-9 h in healthy young subjects; elderly values may be longer.

notes: This is PK time-course information, not a recommendation for anticoagulation monitoring.
Anticoagulant activity assays are distinct from routine plasma concentration monitoring.
Anticoagulant effect and laboratory interpretation are assay- and context-dependent.

tdm.monitoringType: not routinely monitored by plasma concentration
tdm.reason: Routine dose adjustment is not based on plasma concentration; selected anti-Xa or drug-calibrated testing may be used in specific clinical scenarios.
interpretationCaveats: Assay interpretation depends on timing after dose, renal/hepatic function, indication, dose context, and local assay calibration.
This entry is for learning and indexing only, not anticoagulation management.
Routine plasma concentration monitoring is not standard.
Drug-specific calibrated anti-Xa or dilute thrombin/ecarin-based assays are reserved for selected clinical scenarios.

toxicities: Clinically important toxicity is bleeding; risk depends strongly on renal function, age, interacting drugs, and procedure context.
Routine plasma concentration monitoring is not typical, but dilute thrombin time, ecarin-based assays, or other coagulation tests may be used in selected scenarios depending on local availability.
substances.summary: 达比加群酯是直接口服抗凝药，经转化为达比加群发挥直接凝血酶抑制作用。本条目区分药代时间进程和抗凝检测解释。

indications: Stroke/systemic embolism risk reduction in selected atrial fibrillation contexts
Treatment or prevention of venous thromboembolism depending on label
contraindications: Mechanical prosthetic heart valves in relevant labels
majorWarnings: Bleeding can be serious or fatal.
Premature discontinuation can increase thrombotic risk in some contexts.
Renal function is central to exposure and safety.
majorInteractions: P-gp inhibitors can increase dabigatran exposure, especially with renal impairment.
P-gp inducers can reduce exposure.
Other anticoagulants, antiplatelets, or NSAIDs can increase bleeding risk.
monitoring: Specialized coagulation assays only in selected scenarios

notes: Half-life is strongly affected by renal function.
This is PK time-course information, not anticoagulation management guidance.
Anticoagulant effect and laboratory interpretation are assay- and context-dependent.

tdm.monitoringType: not routinely monitored by plasma concentration
tdm.reason: Routine dose adjustment is not based on plasma concentration; specialized coagulation assays may be used in selected scenarios.
interpretationCaveats: Assay interpretation depends on timing after dose, renal function, indication, dose context, and local assay calibration.
This entry is for learning and indexing only, not anticoagulation management.
Routine plasma concentration monitoring is not standard.
Drug-specific calibrated anti-Xa or dilute thrombin/ecarin-based assays are reserved for selected clinical scenarios.

substances.name: Rosuvastatin
toxicities: Clinically important toxicity can include myopathy, rhabdomyolysis, liver enzyme elevations, and renal complications in severe muscle injury.
Risk is affected by dose context, renal function, interacting drugs, and predisposing factors; plasma concentration is not routine monitoring.
substances.summary: 瑞舒伐他汀是 HMG-CoA 还原酶抑制剂。本条目用于学习药代参数、降脂治疗监测指标和相互作用风险索引。

indications: Hyperlipidemia and selected cardiovascular risk-reduction contexts depending on local label
contraindications: Active liver disease in relevant labels
Pregnancy-related contraindications vary by label and jurisdiction
majorWarnings: Myopathy and rhabdomyolysis risk can increase with interacting drugs, renal impairment, and other risk factors.
Liver enzyme abnormalities can occur.
Myopathy and rhabdomyolysis risk increases with interacting drugs or higher exposure.
Pregnancy labeling and reproductive context must be checked before use.
majorInteractions: Cyclosporine and some antiviral or transporter-inhibiting drugs can increase rosuvastatin exposure.
Gemfibrozil and other myopathy-risk combinations require caution per label.
monitoring: Creatine kinase when muscle toxicity is suspected

notes: LDL-C lowering is a longer-term pharmacodynamic outcome and should not be equated with plasma Tmax.
Food and morning/evening administration do not meaningfully change AUC in the cited label.
LDL-C response develops over repeated treatment and is distinct from plasma Tmax or elimination half-life.

tdm.reason: Clinical monitoring focuses on lipid response, adverse muscle symptoms, liver enzymes when indicated, renal context, and interacting drugs.
